title: Neil Larsen

[Soul Survivors](/wiki/Soul_Survivors_(band) "Soul Survivors (band)"), Full Moon, The Larsen-Feiten Band

(born August 7, 1948) is an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz")/[jazz fusion](/wiki/Jazz_fusion "Jazz fusion") keyboardist, musical arranger and composer. He was born in [Cleveland, Ohio](/wiki/Cleveland,_Ohio "Cleveland, Ohio") and grew up in [Sarasota, Florida](/wiki/Sarasota,_Florida "Sarasota, Florida") before relocating to New York and then, in 1977, Los Angeles.[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2)
["Class of 1966"](https://www.rhsfame.org/honorees/class-of-1966/). *Riverview High School Hall of Fame*. Retrieved October 22, 2025.

Musical artist

**Neil Larsen** (born August 7, 1948) is an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz")/[jazz fusion](/wiki/Jazz_fusion "Jazz fusion") keyboardist, musical arranger and composer. He was born in [Cleveland, Ohio](/wiki/Cleveland,_Ohio "Cleveland, Ohio") and grew up in [Sarasota, Florida](/wiki/Sarasota,_Florida "Sarasota, Florida") before relocating to New York and then, in 1977, Los Angeles.[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Neil_Larsen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Larsen was born in [Cleveland, Ohio](/wiki/Cleveland,_Ohio "Cleveland, Ohio") and grew up in [Sarasota, Florida](/wiki/Sarasota,_Florida "Sarasota, Florida").[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2) Larsen graduated from [Riverview High School](/wiki/Riverview_High_School_(Sarasota,_Florida) "Riverview High School (Sarasota, Florida)") in 1966.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He learned piano, drawing inspiration from jazz artists [John Coltrane](/wiki/John_Coltrane "John Coltrane"), [Miles Davis](/wiki/Miles_Davis "Miles Davis") and the [Modern Jazz Quartet](/wiki/Modern_Jazz_Quartet "Modern Jazz Quartet"), and from contemporary rock acts.[[4]](#cite_note-Paste-4)

In 1969, he was drafted to serve in the [Vietnam War](/wiki/Vietnam_War "Vietnam War"). During his time in Vietnam, he worked as a band director, co-ordinating musical entertainment for US armed forces personnel. After his discharge, he moved to New York to work as a musician.[[4]](#cite_note-Paste-4)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Neil_Larsen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

While in New York in the early 1970s, Larsen wrote television [jingles](/wiki/Jingle "Jingle") and played on sessions for various recording artists.[[5]](#cite_note-Argyrakis/IE-5) He formed the band Full Moon with jazz guitarist [Buzz Feiten](/wiki/Buzz_Feiten "Buzz Feiten"), and their self-titled debut album was released in 1972. Larsen was briefly a member of the [Soul Survivors](/wiki/Soul_Survivors_(band) "Soul Survivors (band)"). He contributed as keyboardist, writer and arranger on their 1974 self-titled album on the [TSOP](/wiki/TSOP_Records "TSOP Records") label. He began touring as a member of [Gregg Allman](/wiki/Gregg_Allman "Gregg Allman")'s band in 1975.[[4]](#cite_note-Paste-4)

In 1977, Larsen relocated to Los Angeles,[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2) where he played on sessions by producers such as [Tommy LiPuma](/wiki/Tommy_LiPuma "Tommy LiPuma"), [Russ Titelman](/wiki/Russ_Titelman "Russ Titelman") and [Herb Alpert](/wiki/Herb_Alpert "Herb Alpert").[[5]](#cite_note-Argyrakis/IE-5) These projects led to Larsen signing with Alpert's record company, [A&M Records](/wiki/A%26M_Records "A&M Records"),[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2) for which he recorded on the [Horizon](/wiki/Horizon_Records "Horizon Records") label.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Larsen's debut studio album, *Jungle Fever*, was released in September 1978.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Larsen toured the US in support of the release with a band that included Feiten.[[4]](#cite_note-Paste-4)

The title track from his second studio album, *High Gear*, was nominated for the 1980 [Grammy Award for Best Rock Instrumental Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Rock_Instrumental_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Rock Instrumental Performance").[[8]](#cite_note-8) The album peaked at number 153 on the [*Billboard* Top LPs & Tape](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart in the US[[9]](#cite_note-9) and included musical contributions from Feiten, [Michael Brecker](/wiki/Michael_Brecker "Michael Brecker"), [Steve Gadd](/wiki/Steve_Gadd "Steve Gadd") and [Paulinho da Costa](/wiki/Paulinho_da_Costa "Paulinho da Costa").[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2)

Larsen collaborated further with Feiten in the jazz–rock fusion group the Larsen-Feiten Band. A self-titled album *The Larsen-Feiten Band* was released in 1980 on [Warner Bros. Records](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Records "Warner Bros. Records"). He has also recorded and toured with guitarist [Robben Ford](/wiki/Robben_Ford "Robben Ford"),[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2) who contributed to Larsen's 2007 album *Orbit*.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

From 1982 to 1985, he was a member of [Kenny Loggins](/wiki/Kenny_Loggins "Kenny Loggins")' touring band, playing keyboards and synthesizers.

His compositions have also been recorded by [George Benson](/wiki/George_Benson "George Benson") and [Gregg Allman](/wiki/Gregg_Allman "Gregg Allman"), among others.[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2) Larsen took part in [Miles Davis](/wiki/Miles_Davis "Miles Davis")'s *[Rubberband](/wiki/Rubberband_(Miles_Davis_album) "Rubberband (Miles Davis album)")* sessions in 1985–86, which were later released in 2019. His song "Carnival" was later adapted by Davis into the piece "Carnival Time".[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Larsen has worked as a session musician for many rock artists, including [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones"), [George Harrison](/wiki/George_Harrison "George Harrison"), [Kenny Loggins](/wiki/Kenny_Loggins "Kenny Loggins") and [Don McLean](/wiki/Don_McLean "Don McLean").[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2)[[12]](#cite_note-12) He was the pianist and musical arranger for the [20th Century Fox Television](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox_Television "20th Century Fox Television") show *[Boston Legal](/wiki/Boston_Legal "Boston Legal")*,[[2]](#cite_note-JazzLynx-2) and musical director for jazz singer [Al Jarreau](/wiki/Al_Jarreau "Al Jarreau").[[4]](#cite_note-Paste-4)

From 2008, he toured and recorded as a member of [Leonard Cohen](/wiki/Leonard_Cohen "Leonard Cohen")'s band.[[13]](#cite_note-Tatangelo/Herald-Tribune-13) Larsen performed on Cohen's *[Old Ideas](/wiki/Old_Ideas "Old Ideas")* (2012) album and on the singer's [final world tour](/wiki/Old_Ideas_World_Tour "Old Ideas World Tour"), in 2012–13. Cohen regularly introduced him on stage as "today's foremost exponent of the Hammond B-3 organ".[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Neil_Larsen&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Discography")]

* Full Moon, *Full Moon* (Douglas/Epic, 1972)
* *Jungle Fever* (Horizon/A&M, 1978)
* *High Gear* (Horizon/A&M, 1979)
* *[Larsen-Feiten Band](/wiki/Larsen-Feiten_Band "Larsen-Feiten Band")* (Warner Bros., 1980)
* *Full Moon featuring Neil Larsen & Buzz Feiten* (Warner Bros., 1982)
* *Through Any Window* (MCA, 1987)
* *Smooth Talk* (MCA, 1989)
* Full Moon, *Full Moon Live* (Dreamsville [Japan], 2002) – live rec. 1980–83
* *Orbit* (Straight Ahead, 2007)
* *Forlana* (Portico, 2015)
